jcr:content.jcr:description: Start here to learn more about myAbbVie Assist eligibility criteria.
jcr:content.jcr:title: Patient Assistance

text_container_text.name: Text Container Text
text_container_text.text: AbbVie offers programs that may help eligible patients access their prescribed medicines at low or no cost. Whether you're uninsured, underinsured, or facing financial hardship, we're here to support you.

text_container_text.name: Text Container Text
text_container_text.text:
Patients in the U.S. without insurance or those with limited coverage for our medicines may be eligible to receive AbbVie medicines at no cost to them through myAbbVie Assist.

How to Get Started:

1. Check your eligibility

2. Find your medicine(s)

3. Complete and submit an application

custom_title.name: Custom Title
custom_title.title: New patients, check if you're eligible:

text_container_text.name: Text Container Text
text_container_text.text: Find the right support programs for your medicine.

custom_title.name: Custom Title
custom_title.title: For healthcare providers or patients, see medicines and applications:

text_container_text.name: Text Container Text
text_container_text.text: Access a complete list of medicines and patient applications.

text_container_text.name: Text Container Text
text_container_text.text:
Use this login for access to these medicines:

•Creon® (pancrelipase) • Elahere® (mirvetuximab soravtansine-gynx) • Emrelis™ (telisotuzumab vedotin-tllv) • Humira® (adalimumab) • Linzess® (linaclotide) • Mavyret® (glecaprevir/pibrentasvir) • Qulipta® (atogepant) • Rinvoq® (upadacitinib) • Skyrizi® (risankizumab-rzaa) • Ubrelvy® (ubrogepant) • Viberzi® (eluxadoline) • Vraylar® (cariprazine)

text_container_text.name: Text Container Text
text_container_text.text: Don't have a Patient Portal account? Sign up

text_container_text.name: Text Container Text
text_container_text.text:
AbbVie is committed to helping patients get the medicines they need. That’s why we offer patient assistance programs that provide free AbbVie medicines to qualifying patients. Our Patient Assistance Programs are intended for people that live in the United States, have limited or no health insurance coverage and demonstrate qualifying financial need. In 2025, we assisted more than 210,000 people.

Applying to myAbbVie Assist is simple. It is free to apply, and those who qualify will receive their medicine for free: no co-pays or shipping costs.

accordion_item.name: Accordion Item
accordion_item.text:
AbbVie is committed to helping patients get the medicines they need. That’s why we offer patient assistance programs that provide free AbbVie medicines to qualifying patients. Our Patient Assistance Programs are intended for people that live in the United States, have limited or no health insurance coverage and demonstrate qualifying financial need. In 2025, we assisted more than 210,000 people.

Applying to myAbbVie Assist is simple. It is free to apply, and those who qualify will receive their medicine for free - no co-pays or shipping costs.

This program supports patients who:

Are being treated by a licensed U.S. health care provider on an outpatient basis and prescribed an AbbVie medicine that is included in our assistance program
Have limited or no health insurance coverage
Demonstrate qualifying financial need
Live in the United States
Patients with commercial insurance plans requiring them to apply to myAbbVie Assist as a condition of, requirement for, or prerequisite to coverage of relevant AbbVie products commonly know as alternate funding programs, are not eligible for myAbbVie Assist.

accordion_item_1668152891.name: Accordion Item
accordion_item_1668152891.text: To see a complete list of medicines visit our Available Programs page.

accordion_item_1671969932.name: Accordion Item
accordion_item_1671969932.text:
1. Find your medicine from this list of AbbVie treatments and review eligibility information for myAbbVie Assist.  

Eligibility for myAbbVie Assist may be initiated through AbbVie Patient Access Support, which will review financial support opportunities for all AbbVie programs, including myAbbVie Assist.  

New in July 2024: Patients with Medicare Part D who are under 150% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) must provide proof of denial from the Medicare Extra Help Program also known as Low Income Subsidy (LIS) before evaluation for PAP eligibility.

2. If you would like to apply, complete the provided application with your health care provider and return it to us.  

3. We will review your application and update you and your health care provider about the status.

accordion_item_551664226.name: Accordion Item
accordion_item_551664226.text: If you have questions about myAbbVie Assist, visit the FAQs or call us at 1-800-222-6885.

quote.name: Quote
quote.quotation: Thanks again for supporting me and my health.
